description:
## Problem

Three context files in `.automaker/context/` document the health scanning and scoring system with outdated specifications. The files reference a 7-dimension system (Accessibility, CEM Completeness, Test Coverage, Storybook, TypeScript, Bundle Size, Documentation) with weights totaling 100%, while the actual codebase implements a 14-dimension, 3-tier system with 105 total weight. This misalignment causes:

- Agents receive incorrect dimension priorities and scopes
- Calibration workflows target thresholds for non-existent dimensions
- Delegation routing maps to only 7 specialist roles instead of 14
- Audit protocols omit 7 critical dimensions (CEM-Source Fidelity, API Surface Quality, CSS Architecture, Event Architecture, Slot Architecture, Naming Consistency, Performance, Drupal Readiness)
- New scanners added post-documentation remain undocumented

## Current State

**Files affected:**
- `health-scanning-audit-workflow.md`: Documents 7 dimensions in a simple flat structure with equal-weight tiers
- `scoring-calibration-workflow.md`: Provides thresholds for only 7 dimensions; missing new dimensions entirely
- `custom-agent-delegation.md`: Maps scanner implementations to 7 agents; 7 new scanners lack delegation routing

**Existing dimension list (in files):**
| Dimension | Weight | Owner |
|---|---|---|
| Accessibility compliance | 20% | accessibility-engineer |
| CEM completeness | 20% | technical-writer |
| Test coverage | 15% | test-architect |
| Storybook coverage | 15% | storybook-specialist |
| TypeScript strictness | 15% | typescript-specialist |
| Bundle size | 10% | performance-engineer |
| Documentation | 5% | technical-writer |

## Desired State

**Three-tier system with 14 dimensions and 105 weight:**

**Critical Tier (5 dimensions, 45 weight):**
- CEM Completeness: 15 weight → technical-writer
- Accessibility Compliance: 10 weight → accessibility-engineer
- Type Coverage: 10 weight → typescript-specialist
- Test Coverage: 10 weight → test-architect
- CEM-Source Fidelity: 10 weight → technical-writer (verifies CEM vs. source code sync)

**Important Tier (7 dimensions, 40 weight):**
- API Surface Quality: 10 weight → solutions-architect (method names, parameter patterns, return types)
- CSS Architecture: 5 weight → performance-engineer (Shadow DOM styling API, part exposure, token coverage)
- Event Architecture: 5 weight → solutions-architect (event naming, detail shape consistency, bubbling correctness)
- Slot Architecture: 5 weight → component-architect (slot naming clarity, composition patterns)
- Bundle Size: 5 weight → performance-engineer
- Story Coverage: 5 weight → storybook-specialist (renamed from "Storybook coverage" for clarity)
- Naming Consistency: 5 weight → technical-writer (tag names, attribute/property naming conventions, event prefixes)

**Advanced Tier (2 dimensions, 10 weight):**
- Performance: 5 weight → performance-engineer (render time, memory footprint)
- Drupal Readiness: 5 weight → cto (Drupal integration, Form-associated custom element support, ARIA data attributes)

## Changes Required in Each File

### health-scanning-audit-workflow.md

**Update "Health Dimensions and Owners" table:**
- Replace 7-row table with complete 14-dimension table organized by tier
- Add weight column (absolute, not percentage)
- Group by Critical/Important/Advanced tiers with subtotal rows
- Introduce new owner agents: `component-architect`, `solutions-architect` (for API and Event dimensions)

**Update "Audit Protocol" Phase 2 (Manual Spot-Check):**
- Add verification steps for 7 new dimensions:
  - **CEM-Source Fidelity**: Source-to-manifest comparison for sampled components (syntax match, method visibility, parameter accuracy)
  - **API Surface Quality**: Method signature consistency (naming patterns, parameter orders, return types); comparison across similar components
  - **CSS Architecture**: Shadow DOM styling API audit (parts existence, CSS custom property coverage, `:host()` pattern consistency)
  - **Event Architecture**: Event naming convention audit, detail shape standardization, composed/bubbling flag consistency
  - **Slot Architecture**: Slot naming clarity (vs. implementation patterns), composition example validation
  - **Naming Consistency**: Tag name patterns, attribute/property naming, event prefix consistency
  - **Performance**: Render time benchmarks (< 5ms first paint), memory profiling (< 50KB per instance)
  - **Drupal Readiness**: Form-associated CElem support check, ARIA data attribute coverage, Drupal module integration readiness

**Update Phase 3 (Discrepancy Report):**
- Expand variance thresholds per tier:
  - Critical dimensions: acceptable variance ±2%
  - Important dimensions: acceptable variance ±3%
  - Advanced dimensions: acceptable variance ±5%

**Update Phase 4 (Aggregate Score Validation):**
- Change aggregation formula from simple percentage average to weighted sum: (score₁×15 + score₂×10 + ... + score₁₄×5) / 105
- Verify dimension weights are used correctly in aggregation code

### scoring-calibration-workflow.md

**Update "Weight Verification" section:**
- Change expected total from 100% to 105 weight
- List all 14 dimensions with tier affiliation
- Add rule: "Weights must sum to exactly 105 (15+10+10+10+10+10+5+5+5+5+5+5+5+5)"

**Expand "Threshold Review Checklist" to cover all 14 dimensions:**

*Critical Tier:*
- **CEM Completeness** (existing, refine): Add penalty for non-matching source (→ CEM-Source Fidelity dimension)
- **Accessibility**: (existing, unchanged)
- **Type Coverage**: New thresholds: ≥95% covered → 100; 90–94% → 80–99; 80–89% → 40–79; <80% → 0–39; all-any types → 0
- **Test Coverage**: (existing, refine to use 90%+ = 100, not interpolated scaling)
- **CEM-Source Fidelity**: New dimension — 0 mismatches → 100; 1–2 minor mismatches → 80–99; 3–5 mismatches → 40–79; >5 → 0

*Important Tier:*
- **API Surface Quality**: New thresholds: All public methods documented + parameter names match property names → 100; 1–2 inconsistencies → 70–99; 3+ inconsistencies → 0
- **CSS Architecture**: New thresholds: All internal sections expose ::part() equivalents, CSS custom properties document every style → 100; 50% coverage → 50; <50% → 0
- **Event Architecture**: New thresholds: All events use consistent prefix, detail shape matches schema, all marked composed:true → 100; partial consistency → 50; none → 0
- **Slot Architecture**: New thresholds: Slot names clearly document purpose, examples in JSDoc, default slot present/absent matches use case → 100; unclear naming → 50; no slot docs → 0
- **Bundle Size**: (existing, refine to gzip size only, exclude source maps)
- **Story Coverage**: (rename from Storybook) — All variants + interactions documented → 100; variants only (no interactions) → 60; <50% variants → 0
- **Naming Consistency**: New thresholds: Consistent prefix, property/attribute names follow pattern, events use prefix, methods are verbs → 100; 1–2 violations → 70; 3+ violations → 0

*Advanced Tier:*
- **Performance**: New thresholds: First render <5ms, memory per instance <50KB → 100; <20ms, <200KB → 50; >20ms or >200KB → 0
- **Drupal Readiness**: New thresholds: Full Form-associated support, ARIA data attributes present, Drupal module integration tested → 100; partial support → 50; none → 0

**Update Phase 2 (Distribution Analysis):**
- Define separate healthy distribution ranges for each tier (Critical: stricter; Advanced: more lenient)
- Example: Critical dimensions mean 65–80; Important dimensions mean 60–80; Advanced dimensions mean 50–75

### custom-agent-delegation.md

**Update "MCP Tool Routing" table:**
- Change from 7 tools to 14 tools (one per dimension)
- Add new tools: `scan_cem_source_fidelity`, `scan_api_surface`, `scan_css_architecture`, `scan_event_architecture`, `scan_slot_architecture`, `scan_naming_consistency`, `scan_performance`, `scan_drupal_readiness`
- Assign reviewer for each new tool (map to owner agent from tier table)

**Update "Delegation Routing Table" → new row group "Health Dimension Work":**
- Expand from 7 rows to 14 rows, grouped by tier
- Add new entries for new specialist roles:
  - `component-architect` — handles Slot Architecture dimension (design decisions on slot patterns)
  - `solutions-architect` — handles API Surface Quality and Event Architecture dimensions (cross-component API patterns)
  - `cto` — handles Drupal Readiness (architectural sign-off for platform integration)

**Update "Escalation Path":**
- Add tier-based escalation: Critical dimension bugs → Tier 2 (senior-code-reviewer); Important dimension changes → Tier 1; Advanced → follow-up within 2 weeks

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `health-scanning-audit-workflow.md`: Table updated to list all 14 dimensions with weights; audit protocol Phase 2 includes verification steps for all 14 dimensions; variance thresholds defined by tier (Critical ±2%, Important ±3%, Advanced ±5%); Phase 4 aggregation formula references correct weight total (105)
- [ ] `scoring-calibration-workflow.md`: Weight sum verification checks for 105; threshold review checklist covers all 14 dimensions with numeric ranges; distribution analysis includes per-tier healthy ranges; dimension-specific red flags documented
- [ ] `custom-agent-delegation.md`: MCP tool routing table lists all 14 tools with correct reviewer assignments; delegation routing table expands to include `component-architect` and `solutions-architect`; escalation path includes tier-based routing
- [ ] All three files reference dimensions by both Common Name and Code Name (e.g., "CEM-Source Fidelity" / `cem_source_fidelity`)
- [ ] Cross-references between files are consistent: same dimension name, same weight, same owner agent
- [ ] No implied requirements invented: only changes stated in problem description or necessary to align with 14-dimension system are included
title: Align audit workflow docs with 14-dimension scoring system

descriptionHistory.description:
## Problem

The audit workflow documentation in `.automaker/context/` still references the **old 7-dimension system** (Accessibility 20%, CEM Completeness 20%, Test Coverage 15%, Storybook 15%, TypeScript 15%, Bundle Size 10%, Documentation 5%) while the actual codebase has evolved to a **14-dimension, 3-tier system** with 105 total weight.

### Files that need updating:
- `.automaker/context/health-scanning-audit-workflow.md` — references 7 dimensions
- `.automaker/context/scoring-calibration-workflow.md` — calibration thresholds for old dimensions
- `.automaker/context/custom-agent-delegation.md` — delegation matrix maps to 7 dimensions

### The actual 14 dimensions:
**Critical (5, 45 weight):** CEM Completeness (15), Accessibility (10), Type Coverage (10), Test Coverage (10), CEM-Source Fidelity (10)
**Important (7, 40 weight):** API Surface Quality (10), CSS Architecture (5), Event Architecture (5), Slot Architecture (5), Bundle Size (5), Story Coverage (5), Naming Consistency (5)
**Advanced (2, 10 weight):** Performance (5), Drupal Readiness (5)

## Task
Update all three context files to accurately reflect the 14-dimension system. Map each dimension to its owner agent. Update calibration thresholds. Ensure the audit protocol covers all 14 dimensions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All context files reference the correct 14 dimensions with correct weights
- [ ] Each dimension mapped to its specialist agent
- [ ] Calibration thresholds updated for new dimensions (CEM-Source Fidelity, Slot Architecture, Naming Consistency, etc.)
- [ ] Audit trigger points and variance thresholds reviewed
